lede: A look at September 30 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Olivier Giroud and Marion Cotillard, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Boeing 747
events.desc: The Boeing 747 is rolled out and shown to the public for the first time.

events.title: Botswana
events.desc: Bechuanaland declares its independence, and becomes the Republic of Botswana.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: General Władysław Sikorski becomes prime minister of the Polish government-in-exile.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Radoje Ljutovac becomes the first soldier in history to shoot down an enemy aircraft with ground-to-air fire.

events.title: Georges Bizet
events.desc: Georges Bizet's opera Les pêcheurs de perles, premieres in Paris.

events.title: The Magic Flute
events.desc: The first performance of Mozart's opera The Magic Flute takes place two months before his death.

events.title: Battle of Morlaix
events.desc: Battle of Morlaix is fought in the Hundred Years' War.

events.title: 1139 Ganja earthquake
events.desc: A magnitude 7.7 earthquake strikes the Caucasus mountains in the Seljuk Empire, causing mass destruction and killing up to 300,000 people.

events.title: AD 737
events.desc: The Turgesh drive back an Umayyad invasion of Khuttal, follow them south of the Oxus, and capture their baggage train.

births.name: Nora Stanton Barney
births.line: American civil engineer, architect, and suffragist

births.name: Elie Wiesel
births.line: Romanian-American author, academic, and activist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Shintaro Ishihara
births.line: Japanese author, playwright, and politician, Governor of Tokyo

deaths.text: Leszek II the Black, Polish prince, Duke of Łęczyca, Sieradz, Kraków, Sandomierz

observances.text: Blasphemy Day, educates individuals and groups about blasphemy laws and defends freedom of expression
